project_overview.project_name: Integrated Flood Resilience and Adaptation Project (IFRAP)
project_overview.project_development_objective: To improve livelihoods and essential services and enhance flood risk protection in selected communities affected by the 2022 floods
project_overview.beneficiaries: Approximately 2.7 million people in selected communities affected by 2022 floods in Balochistan province; direct beneficiaries include 1.8 million inhabitants from infrastructure rehabilitation, 640,000 from hydromet services, 280,000 from housing grants, 1.5 million from housing technical assistance, and 640,000 from livelihood support

primary_locations: Selected tehsils in most affected districts
geographic_scope.fragile_or_conflict_affected_areas: Yes - Balochistan is affected by security risks; project includes security management plan; current security situation may restrict travel to project areas; project is marked as 'Fragile within a non-fragile Country' and 'Responding to Natural or Man-made Disaster'

key_components.description: Rehabilitation of priority community infrastructure damaged by floods including irrigation and flood protection infrastructure, water supply schemes, roads, bridges, and small community facilities in calamity-declared districts. Includes sub-components for irrigation/flood control (US$25M), water supply restoration (US$10M), roads/bridges reconstruction (US$10M), and small community facilities (US$5M). Build-back-better approach with improved resilience standards.

Improvement of Pakistan Meteorological Department (PMD) capability to generate and utilize hydromet information for decision-making. Includes modernization of observation infrastructure, data management, ICT systems, weather forecasting, and technical assistance for institutional strengthening and capacity building. Expands networks throughout country with focus on western regions including Balochistan.

Financing of resilient housing reconstruction grants to beneficiaries for reconstruction of core housing units damaged by floods and institutional strengthening. Includes beneficiary-driven reconstruction grants (US$60M) for approximately 35,100 housing units and technical assistance for damage assessment, eligibility verification, GIS-enabled MIS, community mobilization, training, and housing standards development (US$15M). Multi-hazard resistant design.

Provision of livelihood grants to smallholder farmers and agribusinesses for enhancing agricultural and livestock-based livelihoods and community grants for watershed restoration. Includes matching grants for agriculture/livestock production and community grants for watershed restoration with soil/water conservation, vegetative protection, agro-forestry, and rangeland rehabilitation. Promotes climate-smart agriculture.

key_components.component_name: Project Management, Technical Assistance, and Institutional Strengthening
key_components.description: Support for project management at federal and provincial levels, technical assistance for M&E, Project Supervision and Implementation Assistance, preparation of Series of Projects Phase 2, river basin planning studies, flood modeling, community flood resilience plans, and institutional strengthening including internship programs and water policy development.

Facilitates provision of immediate response to eligible crisis or emergency as needed during implementation. Allows reallocation of project funds to support response and reconstruction following adverse natural events. CERC Operations Manual to be prepared with detailed guidelines on triggering and fund use.

implementation_arrangements.implementing_agency: Ministry of Planning, Development and Special Initiatives (MoPD&SI) at federal level; Balochistan Provincial Planning and Development Department (P&DD) at provincial level. Specific implementation: BIWRMDP PIU (Component 1), PMD PIU (Component 2), FPMU (Component 3), BLEP PIU (Component 4), FPMU (Component 5)
implementation_arrangements.coordination_mechanisms: Project Steering Committee (PSC) co-chaired by Federal Minister MoPD&SI and Chief Minister GoB; includes representatives from federal and provincial governments, armed forces; meets quarterly or as needed. Federal Project Management Unit (FPMU) at MoPD&SI for overall coordination and M&E. Provincial Coordination Unit (PCU) at Balochistan P&DD for provincial coordination.
implementation_arrangements.procurement_approach: World Bank Procurement Regulations for IPF Borrowers (Fourth Edition, November 2020); streamlined procurement methods for first year including unlimited threshold for requests for quotations, Selection Based on Consultant's Qualifications, reduced bid preparation period, suspension of standstill period, Bid Securing Declaration, up to 40% advance payment, use of UN agencies, and waivers for performance security on small contracts. Simplified Framework Project Procurement Strategy for Development (PPSD) to be finalized. Some provincial procurement procedures allowed subject to Bank conditions. Procurement post-review and prior review as required.
implementation_arrangements.monitoring_and_evaluation: FPMU responsible for overall M&E and reporting to World Bank. Supported by M&E consultancy and third-party verification agent. GIS-based Management Information System (MIS) established as backbone of M&E and decision-making. Two separate but interoperable MIS systems: one for housing reconstruction and one for other components. Geo-Enabling initiative for Monitoring and Supervision (GEMS) used for remote supervision and real-time safeguards monitoring. Multi-donor platform ME3F for monitoring reconstruction activities. Baseline data gathering financed. PIUs prepare bi-annual progress reports. Third-party verification for infrastructure, fund flows, and E&S compliance. Semi-annual Interim Unaudited Financial Reports (IUFRs) and annual audited financial statements required.

identified_risks.risk_description: Federal and provincial governments show strong interest but implementation may slow in run-up to general elections. Political uncertainty increased due to nation-wide protests and tension between political party and GoP. Province's security risks may impact implementation. Risk of political capture regarding infrastructure rehabilitation.
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Project Steering Committee oversight to promote coordination and collaboration. Regular meetings with federal and provincial counterparts and WB management. Defined framework for selection and appraisal of infrastructure investments with clear selection criteria to mitigate political capture risk.

identified_risks.risk_description: COVID-19 and 2022 floods have weakened stabilization efforts and structural reforms. Risks include worsening external conditions, further natural disasters, slowdown or reversal of policy adjustment before elections. May lead to macroeconomic instability impacting economic activities, prices, household incomes, and project results.
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Substantial price contingencies included in final project cost estimates. Most project interventions are community-level activities (labor-intensive works) less susceptible to macroeconomic instability.

identified_risks.risk_description: National water and climate strategies identify need for provincial-level tailored policies but Balochistan lacks sub-national climate policies, strategies, and action plans. No clear implementation mechanism for climate action projects or dedicated laws/policies for adequate resource budgeting.
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Project includes technical assistance and consensus building to support GoB in finalizing and approving provincial water policy and water law tailored to local context.

identified_risks.risk_description: MoPD&SI and Balochistan P&DD and Irrigation Department have limited understanding and experience in designing and managing complex multisectoral reconstruction projects. Data, information, and knowledge on hydrological, meteorological, and climatological conditions are unreliable or not granular enough for spatially coordinated design.
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Technical assistance through Project Supervision and Implementation Assistance (PSIA) and pool of experts. Capacity building to improve data collection through innovative means including remote sensing. Bank mobilizes additional expertise through Bank Executed Trust Fund. Global Facility for Disaster Reduction and Recovery grant for remote-sensing technology support.

identified_risks.risk_category: Institutional Capacity for Implementation and Sustainability
identified_risks.risk_description: FPMU and PIUs need support to coordinate with different focal points/departments for successful multisectoral project implementation. While regular staff at existing PIUs will be used for sustainable capacity improvement, specialized skills needed to address implementation gaps.
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Project supports use of specialized skills to augment existing staff. Project supports strengthening technical, operational, and financial capacities of implementing entities. Facilitate social mobilization at community level.

identified_risks.risk_description: Project implemented across various departments with FM arrangements managed by established PIUs. Provincial PIUs have adequate FM arrangements but PMD and FPMU PIUs still to be established. Risk of funds not reaching intended beneficiaries.
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Hiring/engaging qualified Financial Management Specialist per Bank-acceptable terms of reference. Adherence to internal control procedures. Third-party verification for fund use and intended purposes. Capacity strengthening of implementing entities. ME3F platform for Bank oversight. Computerized verification system for cash support to beneficiaries. Biometric-based verification. Internal audit firm to conduct audits twice yearly.

identified_risks.risk_description: Key E&S risks include exclusion of potential beneficiaries, elite capture, land use pollution, land conflicts, gender-based violence (GBV), and sexual exploitation/abuse (SEA/SH). Civil works-related environmental risks (pollution, nuisance, worker OHS, community health/safety). Dam safety risks. Risks related to lack of owner-builder capacity. SEA/SH risk rated substantial for housing reconstruction and livelihood components. Risks of misidentification of beneficiaries, incomplete surveying, poor records management leading to exclusion of vulnerable groups.
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Development, consultation, and application of range of E&S tools. Environmental and Social Management Framework (ESMF) and Resettlement Policy Framework prepared within 60 days of effectiveness. Labor Management Procedures prepared within 60 days. Standalone SEA/SH (GBV) Action Plan with Codes of Conduct, behavioral standards, sensitization/training, awareness raising, GBV service provider recruitment, and responsive GRM. Site-specific ESMP/checklists prior to civil works. Comprehensive social assessment for robust beneficiary targeting. GIS-enabled MIS from project onset. Robust awareness campaign. Active project-level GRM for affected parties to submit complaints. Third-party verification of E&S compliance. Community sensitization and behavior change interventions for GBV prevention. Participatory land adjudication and free legal aid for women asserting property rights.

identified_risks.risk_description: Stakeholders (households, communities, local institutions) weakened by widespread disaster. Multidisciplinary project nature with multiple stakeholders, many facing E&S vulnerability. Inter-institutional coordination and cooperation essential but challenging in emergency contexts. Federal and provincial departments need improved coordination.
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: FPMU established at federal level to facilitate institutional coordination. Strong community engagement strategy and citizen engagement mechanisms for participation in community infrastructure and livelihoods components. Communications strategy to increase community awareness and transparency. Project Steering Committee engages key technical and implementation stakeholders for complementarity and alignment. Annual local flood resilience roundtables (virtual or face-to-face) for information sharing and feedback discussion. Preliminary Stakeholder Engagement Plan prepared and updated during implementation.

identified_risks.risk_description: Current security situation in Balochistan will restrict and may prevent travel to project area by project team. Security risks may impede implementation.
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Security management plan developed for overall project. Security measures identified in plan included in work contracts in sensitive areas.

triggered_safeguard_policies: ESS1: Assessment and Management of Environmental and Social Risks and Impacts
ESS3: Resource Efficiency and Pollution Prevention and Management
ESS5: Land Acquisition, Restrictions on Land Use and Involuntary Resettlement
ESS6: Biodiversity Conservation and Sustainable Management of Living Natural Resources
ESS10: Information Disclosure and Stakeholder Engagement
OP 7.50: Projects on International Waterways (triggered - Indus River, Dasht River, Helmand River, Rakshan/Hamun-i-Mashkel system)
safeguards_and_social.grievance_redress_mechanism: Yes - Project-level GRM established as part of inclusive approach. Socially inclusive, participatory, and transparent GRM for Housing Reconstruction Grants (Component 3). Socially inclusive participatory planning and local monitoring mechanisms for matching grants and community grants (Component 4). Community facilitators ('early warning messengers') for flood early warning systems (Component 2). Beneficiary feedback indicator focusing on grievance redressal added to Results Framework. Communities and individuals can submit complaints to project-level GRM or World Bank's Grievance Redress Service (GRS) and Accountability Mechanism (AM). GRM responsive to SEA/SH complaints. Project required to maintain active GRM throughout implementation. Bank regularly monitors GRM functionality.
safeguards_and_social.gender_considerations: Significant gender focus: Project responds to gap in post-disaster reconstruction assistance for women, people with disabilities, and vulnerable households. Component 1 supports reconstruction/rehabilitation of infrastructure in/around girls' schools with design to minimize school-based violence/abuse (adequate lighting, sanitation facilities, lockable toilet stalls). Community sensitization for GBV prevention, early marriages, and gender-inequitable coping mechanisms. Component 3 enhances women's property ownership through incentivizing joint titling and supporting women property owners in asserting ownership rights. Participatory land adjudication, free legal aid, and protection service referrals. Target: 25% of housing units for female-headed households and households with vulnerable women. Component 4 supports women's access to diversified livelihoods with eligibility criteria for female-headed households, women in high-dependency households, women informal workers (home-based, on/off-farm), female agricultural workers, dairy/livestock workers. Flexible schedules and appropriate venues for women's participation. Livestock sheds rehabilitation and matching grants for female farmers. Target: 40% of livelihood improvements for female-headed households and households with vulnerable women. Component 2 improves women's access to early warning information through gender-differentiated messaging and materials for women with limited literacy. Overall project targets 50% female beneficiaries for essential services and flood protection.
vulnerable_groups: Households with vulnerable women (female informal workers, pregnant/lactating women, widows, single women, women with disabilities, women whose husbands have disabilities, elderly women, minority women)
Poorest/most vulnerable households (identified via NSER data)
Women and children in insecure living environments with limited access to privacy/safe facilities

fcv_relevant_details.conflict_sensitivity: Project marked as 'Fragile within a non-fragile Country' and 'Responding to Natural or Man-made Disaster'. Security situation in Balochistan is a key risk factor. Project includes security management plan with security measures in work contracts for sensitive areas. Current security situation may restrict/prevent travel to project areas. Project design incorporates conflict-sensitive approaches through: (1) Community engagement mechanisms to build social cohesion; (2) Transparent beneficiary selection processes to minimize elite capture and inter-communal disputes; (3) Inclusive targeting of vulnerable groups to reduce marginalization; (4) GBV and SEA/SH prevention through community sensitization and behavior change interventions; (5) Participatory planning processes for community infrastructure and livelihoods; (6) Grievance redress mechanisms responsive to community concerns. Post-flood needs assessment identified inter-communal disputes (10%), child marriages (9%), and GBV (7%) as key protection concerns.
fcv_relevant_details.fragility_context: Balochistan is Pakistan's poorest and least developed province with poverty rate of 70.2% (increased to 81.1% post-floods). Population density very low (35 people/km2 vs national 236). 72% rural population. Multidimensional poverty, high stunting (47.4% vs national 37.6%), low female literacy (26% vs 46% nationally), lowest female labor force participation (12%). Province disproportionately affected by 2022 floods with 500,000+ livestock casualties (63% of national total), harvest failures, food insecurity at 23.4% (highest nationally), damage to 587 health facilities, 2,000+ damaged classrooms. Widespread damage to critical infrastructure (190,000+ housing units damaged, 2,222 km roads damaged, 43 bridges damaged, 456 flood protection/irrigation schemes damaged). Balochistan particularly vulnerable to natural disasters (floods, droughts, heatwaves, wildfires, cyclones, coastal erosion) due to geography, socioeconomic background, and climate change. Recurring floods in 2010, 2011, 2022. Driest province with only 210mm average annual precipitation. Limited preparedness of provincial disaster management system. Weak institutional capacity. Limited understanding of complex multisectoral project management. Unreliable hydrological/meteorological/climatological data. Lacks sub-national climate policies and strategies. No dedicated laws/policies for climate action resource budgeting.
fcv_relevant_details.security_considerations: Current security situation in Balochistan restricts and may prevent travel to project areas by project team. Security risks identified as High. Security management plan developed for overall project. Security measures identified in plan included in work contracts in sensitive areas. Project team will need to manage security constraints during implementation, supervision, and monitoring activities. Remote supervision tools (GEMS) and technology-enabled monitoring will help mitigate security-related access constraints.
fcv_relevant_details.displacement_or_refugees: Yes - Flood-related displacement: Flood water remained stagnant in Sohbatpur, Jhal Magsi, and Jaffarabad districts until March 2023, causing temporary isolation and displacement. National Disaster Management Authority and Provincial Disaster Management Authority established evacuation camps for displaced persons. Project addresses displacement through: (1) Housing reconstruction grants for 35,100 housing units to restore shelter; (2) Livelihood support for 80,000 households to restore income; (3) Infrastructure rehabilitation to restore essential services and connectivity. Resettlement Policy Framework prepared to address potential land acquisition and involuntary resettlement risks under ESS5. Framework includes criteria to avoid areas of disputed land tenure and areas subject to recent forced evictions (cut-off 2020). Participatory land adjudication and verification processes support vulnerable individuals in asserting property rights. Free legal aid and protection service referrals available. Project aims universal coverage for all eligible housing units in selected districts to avoid social tensions from selectivity.
